title: Serpocaulon lasiopus
intro: Serpocaulon lasiopus, also known as the 'Lasiopus fern', is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Serpocaulon lasiopus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Serpocaulon lasiopus is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Serpocaulon lasiopus is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Serpocaulon, which consists of various species of epiphytic ferns. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Within the family Polypodiaceae, Serpocaulon lasiopus is classified as a member of the subfamily Polypodioideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
